title: [lichen_l2.c:568-649] SECURITY: lichen_peer_add lacks authentication - peer table poisoning
description:
Security finding: lichen_peer_add() has no authentication mechanism - any caller can add arbitrary peer entries.

Attack scenario:
1. If lichen_peer_add() is called from application layer without authentication
2. Attacker could inject malicious pubkeys that correspond to attacker-controlled private keys
3. Attacker's frames would then be accepted as legitimate

Current state: Not exploitable because no code calls lichen_peer_add() yet.

Future risk: When EDHOC or other key exchange is added, the path from network to lichen_peer_add() must be authenticated end-to-end.

Impact: P2 because requires authenticated application-layer access.

Mitigation:
1. Document that lichen_peer_add() MUST only be called after cryptographic verification of the peer's identity
2. Consider adding a flag/mode to prevent runtime peer additions (factory-provisioned only)
3. Audit all future callers of lichen_peer_add() for proper authentication
close_reason: Documented: architecture limitation - lichen_peer_add() requires caller to authenticate peer identity. Added SECURITY comment explaining valid callers and attack risk.
